behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the exact files (sessionId.ts, createAuthStore.ts), the functions (getSessionId, consumeSessionId, handleCallbackUrl), the concept of a clearSessionId function, and the URL https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/91819. All specifics tested (function names, file paths, import patterns, comment references) are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the fix: add a way to 'explicitly clear/re-consume the session ID' (requirement 1), call it 'after getting the session ID' in handleCallbackUrl (requirement 2), and include a specific comment (requirement 3). Combined with naming the exact function signature pattern (clearSessionId calling consumeSessionId), this leaves virtually no reasoning for the agent about WHAT to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on the exact function name 'clearSessionId' (test_clear_session_id_exported), exact signature 'export function clearSessionId(): void', exact body containing only 'consumeSessionId()' (test_session_id_function_behavior asserts len(lines)==1), and exact import pattern. An alternative correct fix (e.g., calling consumeSessionId() directly in handleCallbackUrl, or using a differently-named function, or inlining the workaround) would fail these tests despite being equally valid.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail_to_pass tests (test_clear_session_id_exported, test_clear_session_id_imported_in_auth_store, test_clear_session_id_called_in_handle_callback, test_session_id_function_behavior) are ALL text-matching tests using regex on file contents with open(path).read(). None import or execute the code. The pass_to_pass tests do run builds/checks but those verify existing behavior, not the bug fix.

test_not_tautological.reason: All four fail_to_pass tests only check text presence in files. A stub that simply adds the text 'export function clearSessionId(): void { consumeSessionId() }' to sessionId.ts and adds the import/call to createAuthStore.ts passes all tests without any actual runtime behavior verification of the bug fix.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All three requirements from instruction.md are tested: (1) clearSessionId export in sessionId.ts, (2) clearSessionId called after getSessionId in handleCallbackUrl, (3) comment referencing the Next.js issue.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named, have descriptive docstrings, and are clearly separated into pass_to_pass and fail_to_pass categories with comments explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task does not produce structured data (JSON/CSV/API/DB).

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names across instruction.md and test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names both exact files and exact functions to modify (getSessionId, consumeSessionId in sessionId.ts, handleCallbackUrl in createAuthStore.ts), and specifies the exact nature of the fix (add a clearing function, call it after getSessionId). This goes beyond describing what's broken and into prescribing the exact implementation location and approach.
